subject: Physics
title: The Milky Way: Our Cosmic Home
description: One-minute skill: The Milky Way: Our Cosmic Home.

hook.text: You live in the Milky Way—a spiral galaxy containing 200 to 400 billion stars and at least as many planets.

buildup.text: The Milky Way is 100,000 light-years across. Light takes 100,000 years to cross it.

discovery.text: Our Sun sits in the Orion Arm, about 26,000 light-years from the galactic center.

twist.text: At the center lurks Sagittarius A*, a supermassive black hole four million times the mass of our Sun.

climax.text: The Milky Way is on a collision course with the Andromeda galaxy. They'll merge in about 4.5 billion years.

punchline.text: 400 billion stars. One of trillions of galaxies. Home is bigger than you think.

quiz.question: What sits at the center of the Milky Way?
options: A supermassive black hole called Sagittarius A*
A cluster of bright young stars
